what_it_does: Stores a sent channel message from claude_code in the dragnet-development channel that welcomes the user and explains how their comments will be harvested, classified, resolved, and reported back.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, address, type=message, source=claude_code, state=sent, title, author_session, channel, empty thread and links, plus created/updated timestamps and a history transition
The body is a direct-line welcome message plus a numbered 5-step protocol for handling user comments
It claims 46 comments have already been collected and are intact
It references a prior synthesis item named 'Obsidian-as-host — synthesis' available in a menu
Created and updated timestamps are identical, matching the single 'filed' history event
standouts: An explicit 5-step comment-to-action contract is embedded in a welcome message
The message promises a coverage ledger that maps every single comment to a visible action
conventions: Follows the channel-memory noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter and Markdown body
Records state history with from/to/by/ts/note fields
notes: A welcome/onboarding message that doubles as a public contract for how user feedback will be tracked and resolved.
questions: What is the 'menu' that contains the 'Obsidian-as-host — synthesis' item?
Where and in what form are the 46 referenced comments stored?
What does 'block by block' refer to in this workflow?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose (welcome the user and explain comment use) matches the file, which is a sent message doing exactly that.
intention_signals: To establish trust and clarity by making the comment-handling pipeline explicit, and to point the user to a related prior synthesis without re-deriving it.
novelty: A welcome message is used as a formal, enumerated contract for comment resolution and coverage accountability.
contribution: A user-facing statement of the comment-to-action workflow and the coverage commitment, which a fused system can keep as the description of how feedback is handled in this channel.
summary_for_embedding: A sent noticeboard message in the dragnet-development channel from claude_code welcoming the user and detailing a five-step protocol for harvesting, classifying, resolving, and ledgering comments.
intention_for_embedding: To onboard the user and make explicit how their scarce input will be converted into traceable actions and replies.
